chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] access control policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the access control policy and the associated access controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the access control policy and procedures; and Review and update the current access control: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2: Account Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2: Account Management
Define and document the types of accounts allowed and specifically prohibited for use within the system; Assign account managers; Require [organization-defined parameter] for group and role membership; Specify: Authorized users of the system; Group and role membership; and Access authorizations (i.e., privileges) and [organization-defined parameter] for each account; Require approvals by [organization-defined parameter] for requests to create accounts; Create, enable, modify, disable, and remove accounts in accordance with [organization-defined parameter]; Monitor the use of accounts; Notify account managers and [organization-defined parameter] within: [organization-defined parameter] when accounts are no longer required; [organization-defined parameter] when users are terminated or transferred; and [organization-defined parameter] when system usage or need-to-know changes for an individual; Authorize access to the system based on: A valid access authorization; Intended system usage; and [organization-defined parameter]; Review accounts for compliance with account management requirements [organization-defined parameter]; Establish and implement a process for changing shared or group account authenticators (if deployed) when individuals are removed from the group; and Align account management processes with personnel termination and transfer processes.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.1: Automated System Account Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.1: Automated System Account Management
Support the management of system accounts using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.2: Automated Temporary and Emergency Account Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.2: Automated Temporary and Emergency Account Management
Automatically [organization-defined parameter] temporary and emergency accounts after [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.3: Disable Accounts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.3: Disable Accounts
Disable accounts within [organization-defined parameter] when the accounts: Have expired; Are no longer associated with a user or individual; Are in violation of organizational policy; or Have been inactive for [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.4: Automated Audit Actions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.4: Automated Audit Actions
Automatically audit account creation, modification, enabling, disabling, and removal actions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.5: Inactivity Logout
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.5: Inactivity Logout
Require that users log out when [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.6: Dynamic Privilege Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.6: Dynamic Privilege Management
Implement [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.7: Privileged User Accounts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.7: Privileged User Accounts
Establish and administer privileged user accounts in accordance with [organization-defined parameter]; Monitor privileged role or attribute assignments; Monitor changes to roles or attributes; and Revoke access when privileged role or attribute assignments are no longer appropriate.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.8: Dynamic Account Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.8: Dynamic Account Management
Create, activate, manage, and deactivate [organization-defined parameter] dynamically.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.9: Restrictions on Use of Shared and Group Accounts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.9: Restrictions on Use of Shared and Group Accounts
Only permit the use of shared and group accounts that meet [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.11: Usage Conditions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.11: Usage Conditions
Enforce [organization-defined parameter] for [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.12: Account Monitoring for Atypical Usage
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.12: Account Monitoring for Atypical Usage
Monitor system accounts for [organization-defined parameter] ; and Report atypical usage of system accounts to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.13: Disable Accounts for High-risk Individuals
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-2.13: Disable Accounts for High-risk Individuals
Disable accounts of individuals within [organization-defined parameter] of discovery of [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3: Access Enforcement
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3: Access Enforcement
Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.2: Dual Authorization
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.2: Dual Authorization
Enforce dual authorization for [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.3: Mandatory Access Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.3: Mandatory Access Control
Enforce [organization-defined parameter] over the set of covered subjects and objects specified in the policy, and where the policy: Is uniformly enforced across the covered subjects and objects within the system; Specifies that a subject that has been granted access to information is constrained from doing any of the following; Passing the information to unauthorized subjects or objects; Granting its privileges to other subjects; Changing one or more security attributes (specified by the policy) on subjects, objects, the system, or system components; Choosing the security attributes and attribute values (specified by the policy) to be associated with newly created or modified objects; and Changing the rules governing access control; and Specifies that [organization-defined parameter] may explicitly be granted [organization-defined parameter] such that they are not limited by any defined subset (or all) of the above constraints.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.4: Discretionary Access Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.4: Discretionary Access Control
Enforce [organization-defined parameter] over the set of covered subjects and objects specified in the policy, and where the policy specifies that a subject that has been granted access to information can do one or more of the following: Pass the information to any other subjects or objects; Grant its privileges to other subjects; Change security attributes on subjects, objects, the system, or the system’s components; Choose the security attributes to be associated with newly created or revised objects; or Change the rules governing access control.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.5: Security-relevant Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.5: Security-relevant Information
Prevent access to [organization-defined parameter] except during secure, non-operable system states.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.7: Role-based Access Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.7: Role-based Access Control
Enforce a role-based access control policy over defined subjects and objects and control access based upon [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.8: Revocation of Access Authorizations
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.8: Revocation of Access Authorizations
Enforce the revocation of access authorizations resulting from changes to the security attributes of subjects and objects based on [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.9: Controlled Release
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.9: Controlled Release
Release information outside of the system only if: The receiving [organization-defined parameter] provides [organization-defined parameter] ; and [organization-defined parameter] are used to validate the appropriateness of the information designated for release.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.10: Audited Override of Access Control Mechanisms
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.10: Audited Override of Access Control Mechanisms
Employ an audited override of automated access control mechanisms under [organization-defined parameter] by [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.11: Restrict Access to Specific Information Types
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.11: Restrict Access to Specific Information Types
Restrict access to data repositories containing [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.12: Assert and Enforce Application Access
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.12: Assert and Enforce Application Access
Require applications to assert, as part of the installation process, the access needed to the following system applications and functions: [organization-defined parameter]; Provide an enforcement mechanism to prevent unauthorized access; and Approve access changes after initial installation of the application.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.13: Attribute-based Access Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.13: Attribute-based Access Control
Enforce attribute-based access control policy over defined subjects and objects and control access based upon [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.14: Individual Access
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.14: Individual Access
Provide [organization-defined parameter] to enable individuals to have access to the following elements of their personally identifiable information: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.15: Discretionary and Mandatory Access Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-3.15: Discretionary and Mandatory Access Control
Enforce [organization-defined parameter] over the set of covered subjects and objects specified in the policy; and Enforce [organization-defined parameter] over the set of covered subjects and objects specified in the policy.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4: Information Flow Enforcement
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4: Information Flow Enforcement
Enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system and between connected systems based on [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.1: Object Security and Privacy Attributes
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.1: Object Security and Privacy Attributes
Use [organization-defined parameter] associated with [organization-defined parameter] to enforce [organization-defined parameter] as a basis for flow control decisions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.2: Processing Domains
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.2: Processing Domains
Use protected processing domains to enforce [organization-defined parameter] as a basis for flow control decisions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.3: Dynamic Information Flow Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.3: Dynamic Information Flow Control
Enforce [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.4: Flow Control of Encrypted Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.4: Flow Control of Encrypted Information
Prevent encrypted information from bypassing [organization-defined parameter] by [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.5: Embedded Data Types
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.5: Embedded Data Types
Enforce [organization-defined parameter] on embedding data types within other data types.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.6: Metadata
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.6: Metadata
Enforce information flow control based on [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.7: One-way Flow Mechanisms
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.7: One-way Flow Mechanisms
Enforce one-way information flows through hardware-based flow control mechanisms.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.8: Security and Privacy Policy Filters
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.8: Security and Privacy Policy Filters
Enforce information flow control using [organization-defined parameter] as a basis for flow control decisions for [organization-defined parameter] ; and [organization-defined parameter] data after a filter processing failure in accordance with [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.9: Human Reviews
Enforce the use of human reviews for [organization-defined parameter] under the following conditions: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.10: Enable and Disable Security or Privacy Policy Filters
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.10: Enable and Disable Security or Privacy Policy Filters
Provide the capability for privileged administrators to enable and disable [organization-defined parameter] under the following conditions: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.11: Configuration of Security or Privacy Policy Filters
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.11: Configuration of Security or Privacy Policy Filters

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.12: Data Type Identifiers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.12: Data Type Identifiers
When transferring information between different security domains, use [organization-defined parameter] to validate data essential for information flow decisions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.13: Decomposition into Policy-relevant Subcomponents
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.13: Decomposition into Policy-relevant Subcomponents
When transferring information between different security domains, decompose information into [organization-defined parameter] for submission to policy enforcement mechanisms.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.14: Security or Privacy Policy Filter Constraints
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.14: Security or Privacy Policy Filter Constraints
When transferring information between different security domains, implement [organization-defined parameter] requiring fully enumerated formats that restrict data structure and content.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.15: Detection of Unsanctioned Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.15: Detection of Unsanctioned Information
When transferring information between different security domains, examine the information for the presence of [organization-defined parameter] and prohibit the transfer of such information in accordance with the [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.17: Domain Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.17: Domain Authentication
Uniquely identify and authenticate source and destination points by [organization-defined parameter] for information transfer.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.19: Validation of Metadata
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.19: Validation of Metadata
When transferring information between different security domains, implement [organization-defined parameter] on metadata.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.20: Approved Solutions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.20: Approved Solutions
Employ [organization-defined parameter] to control the flow of [organization-defined parameter] across security domains.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.21: Physical or Logical Separation of Information Flows
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.21: Physical or Logical Separation of Information Flows
Separate information flows logically or physically using [organization-defined parameter] to accomplish [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.22: Access Only
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.22: Access Only
Provide access from a single device to computing platforms, applications, or data residing in multiple different security domains, while preventing information flow between the different security domains.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.23: Modify Non-releasable Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.23: Modify Non-releasable Information
When transferring information between different security domains, modify non-releasable information by implementing [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.24: Internal Normalized Format
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.24: Internal Normalized Format
When transferring information between different security domains, parse incoming data into an internal normalized format and regenerate the data to be consistent with its intended specification.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.25: Data Sanitization
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.25: Data Sanitization
When transferring information between different security domains, sanitize data to minimize [organization-defined parameter] in accordance with [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.26: Audit Filtering Actions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.26: Audit Filtering Actions
When transferring information between different security domains, record and audit content filtering actions and results for the information being filtered.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.27: Redundant/Independent Filtering Mechanisms
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.27: Redundant/Independent Filtering Mechanisms
When transferring information between different security domains, implement content filtering solutions that provide redundant and independent filtering mechanisms for each data type.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.28: Linear Filter Pipelines
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.28: Linear Filter Pipelines
When transferring information between different security domains, implement a linear content filter pipeline that is enforced with discretionary and mandatory access controls.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.29: Filter Orchestration Engines
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.29: Filter Orchestration Engines
When transferring information between different security domains, employ content filter orchestration engines to ensure that: Content filtering mechanisms successfully complete execution without errors; and Content filtering actions occur in the correct order and comply with [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.30: Filter Mechanisms Using Multiple Processes
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.30: Filter Mechanisms Using Multiple Processes
When transferring information between different security domains, implement content filtering mechanisms using multiple processes.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.31: Failed Content Transfer Prevention
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.31: Failed Content Transfer Prevention
When transferring information between different security domains, prevent the transfer of failed content to the receiving domain.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.32: Process Requirements for Information Transfer
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-4.32: Process Requirements for Information Transfer
When transferring information between different security domains, the process that transfers information between filter pipelines: Does not filter message content; Validates filtering metadata; Ensures the content associated with the filtering metadata has successfully completed filtering; and Transfers the content to the destination filter pipeline.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-5: Separation of Duties
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-5: Separation of Duties
Identify and document [organization-defined parameter] ; and Define system access authorizations to support separation of duties.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6: Least Privilege
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6: Least Privilege
Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users (or processes acting on behalf of users) that are necessary to accomplish assigned organizational tasks.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.1: Authorize Access to Security Functions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.1: Authorize Access to Security Functions
Authorize access for [organization-defined parameter] to: [organization-defined parameter] ; and [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.2: Non-privileged Access for Nonsecurity Functions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.2: Non-privileged Access for Nonsecurity Functions
Require that users of system accounts (or roles) with access to [organization-defined parameter] use non-privileged accounts or roles, when accessing nonsecurity functions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.3: Network Access to Privileged Commands
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.3: Network Access to Privileged Commands
Authorize network access to [organization-defined parameter] only for [organization-defined parameter] and document the rationale for such access in the security plan for the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.4: Separate Processing Domains
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.4: Separate Processing Domains
Provide separate processing domains to enable finer-grained allocation of user privileges.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.5: Privileged Accounts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.5: Privileged Accounts
Restrict privileged accounts on the system to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.6: Privileged Access by Non-organizational Users
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.6: Privileged Access by Non-organizational Users
Prohibit privileged access to the system by non-organizational users.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.7: Review of User Privileges
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.7: Review of User Privileges
Review [organization-defined parameter] the privileges assigned to [organization-defined parameter] to validate the need for such privileges; and Reassign or remove privileges, if necessary, to correctly reflect organizational mission and business needs.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.8: Privilege Levels for Code Execution
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.8: Privilege Levels for Code Execution
Prevent the following software from executing at higher privilege levels than users executing the software: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.9: Log Use of Privileged Functions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.9: Log Use of Privileged Functions
Log the execution of privileged functions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.10: Prohibit Non-privileged Users from Executing Privileged Functions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-6.10: Prohibit Non-privileged Users from Executing Privileged Functions
Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
Enforce a limit of [organization-defined parameter] consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user during a [organization-defined parameter] ; and Automatically [organization-defined parameter] when the maximum number of unsuccessful attempts is exceeded.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7.2: Purge or Wipe Mobile Device
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7.2: Purge or Wipe Mobile Device
Purge or wipe information from [organization-defined parameter] based on [organization-defined parameter] after [organization-defined parameter] consecutive, unsuccessful device logon attempts.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7.3: Biometric Attempt Limiting
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7.3: Biometric Attempt Limiting
Limit the number of unsuccessful biometric logon attempts to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7.4: Use of Alternate Authentication Factor
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7.4: Use of Alternate Authentication Factor
Allow the use of [organization-defined parameter] that are different from the primary authentication factors after the number of organization-defined consecutive invalid logon attempts have been exceeded; and Enforce a limit of [organization-defined parameter] consecutive invalid logon attempts through use of the alternative factors by a user during a [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-8: System Use Notification
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-8: System Use Notification
Display [organization-defined parameter] to users before granting access to the system that provides privacy and security notices consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines and state that: Users are accessing a U.S. Government system; System usage may be monitored, recorded, and subject to audit; Unauthorized use of the system is prohibited and subject to criminal and civil penalties; and Use of the system indicates consent to monitoring and recording; Retain the notification message or banner on the screen until users acknowledge the usage conditions and take explicit actions to log on to or further access the system; and For publicly accessible systems: Display system use information [organization-defined parameter] , before granting further access to the publicly accessible system; Display references, if any, to monitoring, recording, or auditing that are consistent with privacy accommodations for such systems that generally prohibit those activities; and Include a description of the authorized uses of the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9: Previous Logon Notification
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9: Previous Logon Notification
Notify the user, upon successful logon to the system, of the date and time of the last logon.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9.1: Unsuccessful Logons
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9.1: Unsuccessful Logons
Notify the user, upon successful logon, of the number of unsuccessful logon attempts since the last successful logon.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9.2: Successful and Unsuccessful Logons
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9.2: Successful and Unsuccessful Logons
Notify the user, upon successful logon, of the number of [organization-defined parameter] during [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9.3: Notification of Account Changes
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9.3: Notification of Account Changes
Notify the user, upon successful logon, of changes to [organization-defined parameter] during [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9.4: Additional Logon Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-9.4: Additional Logon Information
Notify the user, upon successful logon, of the following additional information: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-10: Concurrent Session Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-10: Concurrent Session Control
Limit the number of concurrent sessions for each [organization-defined parameter] to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-11: Device Lock
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-11: Device Lock
Prevent further access to the system by [organization-defined parameter] ; and Retain the device lock until the user reestablishes access using established identification and authentication procedures.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-11.1: Pattern-hiding Displays
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-11.1: Pattern-hiding Displays

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-12: Session Termination
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-12: Session Termination
Automatically terminate a user session after [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-12.1: User-initiated Logouts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-12.1: User-initiated Logouts
Provide a logout capability for user-initiated communications sessions whenever authentication is used to gain access to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-12.2: Termination Message
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-12.2: Termination Message
Display an explicit logout message to users indicating the termination of authenticated communications sessions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-12.3: Timeout Warning Message
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-12.3: Timeout Warning Message
Display an explicit message to users indicating that the session will end in [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-14: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-14: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication
Identify [organization-defined parameter] that can be performed on the system without identification or authentication consistent with organizational mission and business functions; and Document and provide supporting rationale in the security plan for the system, user actions not requiring identification or authentication.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16: Security and Privacy Attributes
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16: Security and Privacy Attributes
Provide the means to associate [organization-defined parameter] with [organization-defined parameter] for information in storage, in process, and/or in transmission; Ensure that the attribute associations are made and retained with the information; Establish the following permitted security and privacy attributes from the attributes defined in [AC-16a](#ac-16_smt.a) for [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter]; Determine the following permitted attribute values or ranges for each of the established attributes: [organization-defined parameter]; Audit changes to attributes; and Review [organization-defined parameter] for applicability [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.1: Dynamic Attribute Association
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.1: Dynamic Attribute Association
Dynamically associate security and privacy attributes with [organization-defined parameter] in accordance with the following security and privacy policies as information is created and combined: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.2: Attribute Value Changes by Authorized Individuals
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.2: Attribute Value Changes by Authorized Individuals
Provide authorized individuals (or processes acting on behalf of individuals) the capability to define or change the value of associated security and privacy attributes.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.3: Maintenance of Attribute Associations by System
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.3: Maintenance of Attribute Associations by System
Maintain the association and integrity of [organization-defined parameter] to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.4: Association of Attributes by Authorized Individuals
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.4: Association of Attributes by Authorized Individuals
Provide the capability to associate [organization-defined parameter] with [organization-defined parameter] by authorized individuals (or processes acting on behalf of individuals).

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.5: Attribute Displays on Objects to Be Output
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.5: Attribute Displays on Objects to Be Output
Display security and privacy attributes in human-readable form on each object that the system transmits to output devices to identify [organization-defined parameter] using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.6: Maintenance of Attribute Association
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.6: Maintenance of Attribute Association
Require personnel to associate and maintain the association of [organization-defined parameter] with [organization-defined parameter] in accordance with [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.7: Consistent Attribute Interpretation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.7: Consistent Attribute Interpretation
Provide a consistent interpretation of security and privacy attributes transmitted between distributed system components.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.8: Association Techniques and Technologies
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.8: Association Techniques and Technologies
Implement [organization-defined parameter] in associating security and privacy attributes to information.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.9: Attribute Reassignment — Regrading Mechanisms
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.9: Attribute Reassignment — Regrading Mechanisms
Change security and privacy attributes associated with information only via regrading mechanisms validated using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.10: Attribute Configuration by Authorized Individuals
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-16.10: Attribute Configuration by Authorized Individuals
Provide authorized individuals the capability to define or change the type and value of security and privacy attributes available for association with subjects and objects.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17: Remote Access
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17: Remote Access
Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed; and Authorize each type of remote access to the system prior to allowing such connections.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.1: Monitoring and Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.1: Monitoring and Control
Employ automated mechanisms to monitor and control remote access methods.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.2: Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.2: Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption
Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality and integrity of remote access sessions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.3: Managed Access Control Points
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.3: Managed Access Control Points
Route remote accesses through authorized and managed network access control points.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.4: Privileged Commands and Access
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.4: Privileged Commands and Access
Authorize the execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only in a format that provides assessable evidence and for the following needs: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Document the rationale for remote access in the security plan for the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.6: Protection of Mechanism Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.6: Protection of Mechanism Information
Protect information about remote access mechanisms from unauthorized use and disclosure.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.9: Disconnect or Disable Access
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.9: Disconnect or Disable Access
Provide the capability to disconnect or disable remote access to the system within [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.10: Authenticate Remote Commands
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-17.10: Authenticate Remote Commands
Implement [organization-defined parameter] to authenticate [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18: Wireless Access
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18: Wireless Access
Establish configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of wireless access; and Authorize each type of wireless access to the system prior to allowing such connections.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18.1: Authentication and Encryption
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18.1: Authentication and Encryption
Protect wireless access to the system using authentication of [organization-defined parameter] and encryption.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18.3: Disable Wireless Networking
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18.3: Disable Wireless Networking
Disable, when not intended for use, wireless networking capabilities embedded within system components prior to issuance and deployment.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18.4: Restrict Configurations by Users
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18.4: Restrict Configurations by Users
Identify and explicitly authorize users allowed to independently configure wireless networking capabilities.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18.5: Antennas and Transmission Power Levels
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-18.5: Antennas and Transmission Power Levels
Select radio antennas and calibrate transmission power levels to reduce the probability that signals from wireless access points can be received outside of organization-controlled boundaries.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-19: Access Control for Mobile Devices
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-19: Access Control for Mobile Devices
Establish configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for organization-controlled mobile devices, to include when such devices are outside of controlled areas; and Authorize the connection of mobile devices to organizational systems.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-19.4: Restrictions for Classified Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-19.4: Restrictions for Classified Information
Prohibit the use of unclassified mobile devices in facilities containing systems processing, storing, or transmitting classified information unless specifically permitted by the authorizing official; and Enforce the following restrictions on individuals permitted by the authorizing official to use unclassified mobile devices in facilities containing systems processing, storing, or transmitting classified information: Connection of unclassified mobile devices to classified systems is prohibited; Connection of unclassified mobile devices to unclassified systems requires approval from the authorizing official; Use of internal or external modems or wireless interfaces within the unclassified mobile devices is prohibited; and Unclassified mobile devices and the information stored on those devices are subject to random reviews and inspections by [organization-defined parameter] , and if classified information is found, the incident handling policy is followed. Restrict the connection of classified mobile devices to classified systems in accordance with [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-19.5: Full Device or Container-based Encryption
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-19.5: Full Device or Container-based Encryption
Employ [organization-defined parameter] to protect the confidentiality and integrity of information on [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20: Use of External Systems
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20: Use of External Systems
[organization-defined parameter] , consistent with the trust relationships established with other organizations owning, operating, and/or maintaining external systems, allowing authorized individuals to: Access the system from external systems; and Process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information using external systems; or Prohibit the use of [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.1: Limits on Authorized Use
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.1: Limits on Authorized Use
Permit authorized individuals to use an external system to access the system or to process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information only after: Verification of the implementation of controls on the external system as specified in the organization’s security and privacy policies and security and privacy plans; or Retention of approved system connection or processing agreements with the organizational entity hosting the external system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.2: Portable Storage Devices — Restricted Use
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.2: Portable Storage Devices — Restricted Use
Restrict the use of organization-controlled portable storage devices by authorized individuals on external systems using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.3: Non-organizationally Owned Systems — Restricted Use
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.3: Non-organizationally Owned Systems — Restricted Use
Restrict the use of non-organizationally owned systems or system components to process, store, or transmit organizational information using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.4: Network Accessible Storage Devices — Prohibited Use
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.4: Network Accessible Storage Devices — Prohibited Use
Prohibit the use of [organization-defined parameter] in external systems.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.5: Portable Storage Devices — Prohibited Use
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-20.5: Portable Storage Devices — Prohibited Use
Prohibit the use of organization-controlled portable storage devices by authorized individuals on external systems.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-21: Information Sharing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-21: Information Sharing
Enable authorized users to determine whether access authorizations assigned to a sharing partner match the information’s access and use restrictions for [organization-defined parameter] ; and Employ [organization-defined parameter] to assist users in making information sharing and collaboration decisions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-21.1: Automated Decision Support
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-21.1: Automated Decision Support
Employ [organization-defined parameter] to enforce information-sharing decisions by authorized users based on access authorizations of sharing partners and access restrictions on information to be shared.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-21.2: Information Search and Retrieval
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-21.2: Information Search and Retrieval
Implement information search and retrieval services that enforce [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-22: Publicly Accessible Content
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-22: Publicly Accessible Content
Designate individuals authorized to make information publicly accessible; Train authorized individuals to ensure that publicly accessible information does not contain nonpublic information; Review the proposed content of information prior to posting onto the publicly accessible system to ensure that nonpublic information is not included; and Review the content on the publicly accessible system for nonpublic information [organization-defined parameter] and remove such information, if discovered.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-23: Data Mining Protection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-23: Data Mining Protection
Employ [organization-defined parameter] for [organization-defined parameter] to detect and protect against unauthorized data mining.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-24: Access Control Decisions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-24: Access Control Decisions
[organization-defined parameter] to ensure [organization-defined parameter] are applied to each access request prior to access enforcement.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-24.1: Transmit Access Authorization Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-24.1: Transmit Access Authorization Information
Transmit [organization-defined parameter] using [organization-defined parameter] to [organization-defined parameter] that enforce access control decisions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-24.2: No User or Process Identity
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-24.2: No User or Process Identity
Enforce access control decisions based on [organization-defined parameter] that do not include the identity of the user or process acting on behalf of the user.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-25: Reference Monitor
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-25: Reference Monitor
Implement a reference monitor for [organization-defined parameter] that is tamperproof, always invoked, and small enough to be subject to analysis and testing, the completeness of which can be assured.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] awareness and training policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the awareness and training policy and the associated awareness and training controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the awareness and training policy and procedures; and Review and update the current awareness and training: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2: Literacy Training and Awareness
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2: Literacy Training and Awareness
Provide security and privacy literacy training to system users (including managers, senior executives, and contractors): As part of initial training for new users and [organization-defined parameter] thereafter; and When required by system changes or following [organization-defined parameter]; Employ the following techniques to increase the security and privacy awareness of system users [organization-defined parameter]; Update literacy training and awareness content [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Incorporate lessons learned from internal or external security incidents or breaches into literacy training and awareness techniques.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.1: Practical Exercises
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.1: Practical Exercises
Provide practical exercises in literacy training that simulate events and incidents.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.2: Insider Threat
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.2: Insider Threat
Provide literacy training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.3: Social Engineering and Mining
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.3: Social Engineering and Mining
Provide literacy training on recognizing and reporting potential and actual instances of social engineering and social mining.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.4: Suspicious Communications and Anomalous System Behavior
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.4: Suspicious Communications and Anomalous System Behavior
Provide literacy training on recognizing suspicious communications and anomalous behavior in organizational systems using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.5: Advanced Persistent Threat
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.5: Advanced Persistent Threat
Provide literacy training on the advanced persistent threat.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.6: Cyber Threat Environment
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-2.6: Cyber Threat Environment
Provide literacy training on the cyber threat environment; and Reflect current cyber threat information in system operations.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3: Role-based Training
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3: Role-based Training
Provide role-based security and privacy training to personnel with the following roles and responsibilities: [organization-defined parameter]: Before authorizing access to the system, information, or performing assigned duties, and [organization-defined parameter] thereafter; and When required by system changes; Update role-based training content [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Incorporate lessons learned from internal or external security incidents or breaches into role-based training.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3.1: Environmental Controls
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3.1: Environmental Controls
Provide [organization-defined parameter] with initial and [organization-defined parameter] training in the employment and operation of environmental controls.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3.2: Physical Security Controls
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3.2: Physical Security Controls
Provide [organization-defined parameter] with initial and [organization-defined parameter] training in the employment and operation of physical security controls.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3.3: Practical Exercises
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3.3: Practical Exercises
Provide practical exercises in security and privacy training that reinforce training objectives.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3.5: Processing Personally Identifiable Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-3.5: Processing Personally Identifiable Information
Provide [organization-defined parameter] with initial and [organization-defined parameter] training in the employment and operation of personally identifiable information processing and transparency controls.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-4: Training Records
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-4: Training Records
Document and monitor information security and privacy training activities, including security and privacy awareness training and specific role-based security and privacy training; and Retain individual training records for [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AT-6: Training Feedback
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AT-6: Training Feedback
Provide feedback on organizational training results to the following personnel [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] audit and accountability policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the audit and accountability policy and the associated audit and accountability controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the audit and accountability policy and procedures; and Review and update the current audit and accountability: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-2: Event Logging
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-2: Event Logging
Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function: [organization-defined parameter]; Coordinate the event logging function with other organizational entities requiring audit-related information to guide and inform the selection criteria for events to be logged; Specify the following event types for logging within the system: [organization-defined parameter]; Provide a rationale for why the event types selected for logging are deemed to be adequate to support after-the-fact investigations of incidents; and Review and update the event types selected for logging [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-3: Content of Audit Records
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-3: Content of Audit Records
Ensure that audit records contain information that establishes the following: What type of event occurred; When the event occurred; Where the event occurred; Source of the event; Outcome of the event; and Identity of any individuals, subjects, or objects/entities associated with the event.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-3.1: Additional Audit Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-3.1: Additional Audit Information
Generate audit records containing the following additional information: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-3.3: Limit Personally Identifiable Information Elements
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-3.3: Limit Personally Identifiable Information Elements
Limit personally identifiable information contained in audit records to the following elements identified in the privacy risk assessment: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-4: Audit Log Storage Capacity
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-4: Audit Log Storage Capacity
Allocate audit log storage capacity to accommodate [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-4.1: Transfer to Alternate Storage
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-4.1: Transfer to Alternate Storage
Transfer audit logs [organization-defined parameter] to a different system, system component, or media other than the system or system component conducting the logging.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
Alert [organization-defined parameter] within [organization-defined parameter] in the event of an audit logging process failure; and Take the following additional actions: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.1: Storage Capacity Warning
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.1: Storage Capacity Warning
Provide a warning to [organization-defined parameter] within [organization-defined parameter] when allocated audit log storage volume reaches [organization-defined parameter] of repository maximum audit log storage capacity.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.2: Real-time Alerts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.2: Real-time Alerts
Provide an alert within [organization-defined parameter] to [organization-defined parameter] when the following audit failure events occur: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.3: Configurable Traffic Volume Thresholds
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.3: Configurable Traffic Volume Thresholds
Enforce configurable network communications traffic volume thresholds reflecting limits on audit log storage capacity and [organization-defined parameter] network traffic above those thresholds.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.4: Shutdown on Failure
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.4: Shutdown on Failure
Invoke a [organization-defined parameter] in the event of [organization-defined parameter] , unless an alternate audit logging capability exists.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.5: Alternate Audit Logging Capability
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-5.5: Alternate Audit Logging Capability
Provide an alternate audit logging capability in the event of a failure in primary audit logging capability that implements [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
Review and analyze system audit records [organization-defined parameter] for indications of [organization-defined parameter] and the potential impact of the inappropriate or unusual activity; Report findings to [organization-defined parameter] ; and Adjust the level of audit record review, analysis, and reporting within the system when there is a change in risk based on law enforcement information, intelligence information, or other credible sources of information.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.1: Automated Process Integration
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.1: Automated Process Integration
Integrate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.3: Correlate Audit Record Repositories
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.3: Correlate Audit Record Repositories
Analyze and correlate audit records across different repositories to gain organization-wide situational awareness.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.4: Central Review and Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.4: Central Review and Analysis
Provide and implement the capability to centrally review and analyze audit records from multiple components within the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.5: Integrated Analysis of Audit Records
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.5: Integrated Analysis of Audit Records
Integrate analysis of audit records with analysis of [organization-defined parameter] to further enhance the ability to identify inappropriate or unusual activity.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.6: Correlation with Physical Monitoring
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.6: Correlation with Physical Monitoring
Correlate information from audit records with information obtained from monitoring physical access to further enhance the ability to identify suspicious, inappropriate, unusual, or malevolent activity.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.7: Permitted Actions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.7: Permitted Actions
Specify the permitted actions for each [organization-defined parameter] associated with the review, analysis, and reporting of audit record information.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.8: Full Text Analysis of Privileged Commands
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.8: Full Text Analysis of Privileged Commands
Perform a full text analysis of logged privileged commands in a physically distinct component or subsystem of the system, or other system that is dedicated to that analysis.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.9: Correlation with Information from Nontechnical Sources
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6.9: Correlation with Information from Nontechnical Sources
Correlate information from nontechnical sources with audit record information to enhance organization-wide situational awareness.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-7: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-7: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
Provide and implement an audit record reduction and report generation capability that: Supports on-demand audit record review, analysis, and reporting requirements and after-the-fact investigations of incidents; and Does not alter the original content or time ordering of audit records.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-7.1: Automatic Processing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-7.1: Automatic Processing
Provide and implement the capability to process, sort, and search audit records for events of interest based on the following content: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-8: Time Stamps
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-8: Time Stamps
Use internal system clocks to generate time stamps for audit records; and Record time stamps for audit records that meet [organization-defined parameter] and that use Coordinated Universal Time, have a fixed local time offset from Coordinated Universal Time, or that include the local time offset as part of the time stamp.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9: Protection of Audit Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9: Protection of Audit Information
Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion; and Alert [organization-defined parameter] upon detection of unauthorized access, modification, or deletion of audit information.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.1: Hardware Write-once Media
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.1: Hardware Write-once Media
Write audit trails to hardware-enforced, write-once media.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.2: Store on Separate Physical Systems or Components
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.2: Store on Separate Physical Systems or Components
Store audit records [organization-defined parameter] in a repository that is part of a physically different system or system component than the system or component being audited.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.3: Cryptographic Protection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.3: Cryptographic Protection
Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity of audit information and audit tools.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.4: Access by Subset of Privileged Users
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.4: Access by Subset of Privileged Users
Authorize access to management of audit logging functionality to only [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.5: Dual Authorization
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.5: Dual Authorization
Enforce dual authorization for [organization-defined parameter] of [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.6: Read-only Access
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.6: Read-only Access
Authorize read-only access to audit information to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.7: Store on Component with Different Operating System
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-9.7: Store on Component with Different Operating System
Store audit information on a component running a different operating system than the system or component being audited.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-10: Non-repudiation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-10: Non-repudiation
Provide irrefutable evidence that an individual (or process acting on behalf of an individual) has performed [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-10.1: Association of Identities
