chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.2: Automated Maintenance
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.2: Automated Maintenance
Maintain the currency, completeness, accuracy, and availability of the inventory of system components using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.3: Automated Unauthorized Component Detection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.3: Automated Unauthorized Component Detection
Detect the presence of unauthorized hardware, software, and firmware components within the system using [organization-defined parameter] [organization-defined parameter] ; and Take the following actions when unauthorized components are detected: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.4: Accountability Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.4: Accountability Information
Include in the system component inventory information, a means for identifying by [organization-defined parameter] , individuals responsible and accountable for administering those components.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.6: Assessed Configurations and Approved Deviations
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.6: Assessed Configurations and Approved Deviations
Include assessed component configurations and any approved deviations to current deployed configurations in the system component inventory.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.7: Centralized Repository
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.7: Centralized Repository
Provide a centralized repository for the inventory of system components.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.8: Automated Location Tracking
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.8: Automated Location Tracking
Support the tracking of system components by geographic location using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.9: Assignment of Components to Systems
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-8.9: Assignment of Components to Systems
Assign system components to a system; and Receive an acknowledgement from [organization-defined parameter] of this assignment.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-9: Configuration Management Plan
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-9: Configuration Management Plan
Develop, document, and implement a configuration management plan for the system that: Addresses roles, responsibilities, and configuration management processes and procedures; Establishes a process for identifying configuration items throughout the system development life cycle and for managing the configuration of the configuration items; Defines the configuration items for the system and places the configuration items under configuration management; Is reviewed and approved by [organization-defined parameter] ; and Protects the configuration management plan from unauthorized disclosure and modification.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-9.1: Assignment of Responsibility
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-9.1: Assignment of Responsibility
Assign responsibility for developing the configuration management process to organizational personnel that are not directly involved in system development.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-10: Software Usage Restrictions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-10: Software Usage Restrictions
Use software and associated documentation in accordance with contract agreements and copyright laws; Track the use of software and associated documentation protected by quantity licenses to control copying and distribution; and Control and document the use of peer-to-peer file sharing technology to ensure that this capability is not used for the unauthorized distribution, display, performance, or reproduction of copyrighted work.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-10.1: Open-source Software
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-10.1: Open-source Software
Establish the following restrictions on the use of open-source software: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-11: User-installed Software
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-11: User-installed Software
Establish [organization-defined parameter] governing the installation of software by users; Enforce software installation policies through the following methods: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Monitor policy compliance [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-11.2: Software Installation with Privileged Status
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-11.2: Software Installation with Privileged Status
Allow user installation of software only with explicit privileged status.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-11.3: Automated Enforcement and Monitoring
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-11.3: Automated Enforcement and Monitoring
Enforce and monitor compliance with software installation policies using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-12: Information Location
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-12: Information Location
Identify and document the location of [organization-defined parameter] and the specific system components on which the information is processed and stored; Identify and document the users who have access to the system and system components where the information is processed and stored; and Document changes to the location (i.e., system or system components) where the information is processed and stored.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-12.1: Automated Tools to Support Information Location
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-12.1: Automated Tools to Support Information Location
Use automated tools to identify [organization-defined parameter] on [organization-defined parameter] to ensure controls are in place to protect organizational information and individual privacy.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-13: Data Action Mapping
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-13: Data Action Mapping
Develop and document a map of system data actions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CM-14: Signed Components
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CM-14: Signed Components
Prevent the installation of [organization-defined parameter] without verification that the component has been digitally signed using a certificate that is recognized and approved by the organization.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] contingency planning policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the contingency planning policy and the associated contingency planning controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the contingency planning policy and procedures; and Review and update the current contingency planning: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2: Contingency Plan
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2: Contingency Plan
Develop a contingency plan for the system that: Identifies essential mission and business functions and associated contingency requirements; Provides recovery objectives, restoration priorities, and metrics; Addresses contingency roles, responsibilities, assigned individuals with contact information; Addresses maintaining essential mission and business functions despite a system disruption, compromise, or failure; Addresses eventual, full system restoration without deterioration of the controls originally planned and implemented; Addresses the sharing of contingency information; and Is reviewed and approved by [organization-defined parameter]; Distribute copies of the contingency plan to [organization-defined parameter]; Coordinate contingency planning activities with incident handling activities; Review the contingency plan for the system [organization-defined parameter]; Update the contingency plan to address changes to the organization, system, or environment of operation and problems encountered during contingency plan implementation, execution, or testing; Communicate contingency plan changes to [organization-defined parameter]; Incorporate lessons learned from contingency plan testing, training, or actual contingency activities into contingency testing and training; and Protect the contingency plan from unauthorized disclosure and modification.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.1: Coordinate with Related Plans
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.1: Coordinate with Related Plans
Coordinate contingency plan development with organizational elements responsible for related plans.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.2: Capacity Planning
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.2: Capacity Planning
Conduct capacity planning so that necessary capacity for information processing, telecommunications, and environmental support exists during contingency operations.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.3: Resume Mission and Business Functions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.3: Resume Mission and Business Functions
Plan for the resumption of [organization-defined parameter] mission and business functions within [organization-defined parameter] of contingency plan activation.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.5: Continue Mission and Business Functions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.5: Continue Mission and Business Functions
Plan for the continuance of [organization-defined parameter] mission and business functions with minimal or no loss of operational continuity and sustains that continuity until full system restoration at primary processing and/or storage sites.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.6: Alternate Processing and Storage Sites
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.6: Alternate Processing and Storage Sites
Plan for the transfer of [organization-defined parameter] mission and business functions to alternate processing and/or storage sites with minimal or no loss of operational continuity and sustain that continuity through system restoration to primary processing and/or storage sites.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.7: Coordinate with External Service Providers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.7: Coordinate with External Service Providers
Coordinate the contingency plan with the contingency plans of external service providers to ensure that contingency requirements can be satisfied.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.8: Identify Critical Assets
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-2.8: Identify Critical Assets
Identify critical system assets supporting [organization-defined parameter] mission and business functions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-3: Contingency Training
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-3: Contingency Training
Provide contingency training to system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities: Within [organization-defined parameter] of assuming a contingency role or responsibility; When required by system changes; and [organization-defined parameter] thereafter; and Review and update contingency training content [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-3.1: Simulated Events
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-3.1: Simulated Events
Incorporate simulated events into contingency training to facilitate effective response by personnel in crisis situations.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-3.2: Mechanisms Used in Training Environments
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-3.2: Mechanisms Used in Training Environments
Employ mechanisms used in operations to provide a more thorough and realistic contingency training environment.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4: Contingency Plan Testing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4: Contingency Plan Testing
Test the contingency plan for the system [organization-defined parameter] using the following tests to determine the effectiveness of the plan and the readiness to execute the plan: [organization-defined parameter]. Review the contingency plan test results; and Initiate corrective actions, if needed.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.1: Coordinate with Related Plans
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.1: Coordinate with Related Plans
Coordinate contingency plan testing with organizational elements responsible for related plans.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.2: Alternate Processing Site
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.2: Alternate Processing Site
Test the contingency plan at the alternate processing site: To familiarize contingency personnel with the facility and available resources; and To evaluate the capabilities of the alternate processing site to support contingency operations.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.3: Automated Testing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.3: Automated Testing
Test the contingency plan using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.4: Full Recovery and Reconstitution
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.4: Full Recovery and Reconstitution
Include a full recovery and reconstitution of the system to a known state as part of contingency plan testing.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.5: Self-challenge
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-4.5: Self-challenge
Employ [organization-defined parameter] to [organization-defined parameter] to disrupt and adversely affect the system or system component.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-6: Alternate Storage Site
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-6: Alternate Storage Site
Establish an alternate storage site, including necessary agreements to permit the storage and retrieval of system backup information; and Ensure that the alternate storage site provides controls equivalent to that of the primary site.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-6.1: Separation from Primary Site
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-6.1: Separation from Primary Site
Identify an alternate storage site that is sufficiently separated from the primary storage site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-6.2: Recovery Time and Recovery Point Objectives
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-6.2: Recovery Time and Recovery Point Objectives
Configure the alternate storage site to facilitate recovery operations in accordance with recovery time and recovery point objectives.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-6.3: Accessibility
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-6.3: Accessibility
Identify potential accessibility problems to the alternate storage site in the event of an area-wide disruption or disaster and outline explicit mitigation actions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7: Alternate Processing Site
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7: Alternate Processing Site
Establish an alternate processing site, including necessary agreements to permit the transfer and resumption of [organization-defined parameter] for essential mission and business functions within [organization-defined parameter] when the primary processing capabilities are unavailable; Make available at the alternate processing site, the equipment and supplies required to transfer and resume operations or put contracts in place to support delivery to the site within the organization-defined time period for transfer and resumption; and Provide controls at the alternate processing site that are equivalent to those at the primary site.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.1: Separation from Primary Site
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.1: Separation from Primary Site
Identify an alternate processing site that is sufficiently separated from the primary processing site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.2: Accessibility
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.2: Accessibility
Identify potential accessibility problems to alternate processing sites in the event of an area-wide disruption or disaster and outlines explicit mitigation actions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.3: Priority of Service
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.3: Priority of Service
Develop alternate processing site agreements that contain priority-of-service provisions in accordance with availability requirements (including recovery time objectives).

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.4: Preparation for Use
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.4: Preparation for Use
Prepare the alternate processing site so that the site can serve as the operational site supporting essential mission and business functions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.6: Inability to Return to Primary Site
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-7.6: Inability to Return to Primary Site
Plan and prepare for circumstances that preclude returning to the primary processing site.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8: Telecommunications Services
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8: Telecommunications Services
Establish alternate telecommunications services, including necessary agreements to permit the resumption of [organization-defined parameter] for essential mission and business functions within [organization-defined parameter] when the primary telecommunications capabilities are unavailable at either the primary or alternate processing or storage sites.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.1: Priority of Service Provisions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.1: Priority of Service Provisions
Develop primary and alternate telecommunications service agreements that contain priority-of-service provisions in accordance with availability requirements (including recovery time objectives); and Request Telecommunications Service Priority for all telecommunications services used for national security emergency preparedness if the primary and/or alternate telecommunications services are provided by a common carrier.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.2: Single Points of Failure
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.2: Single Points of Failure
Obtain alternate telecommunications services to reduce the likelihood of sharing a single point of failure with primary telecommunications services.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.3: Separation of Primary and Alternate Providers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.3: Separation of Primary and Alternate Providers
Obtain alternate telecommunications services from providers that are separated from primary service providers to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.4: Provider Contingency Plan
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.4: Provider Contingency Plan
Require primary and alternate telecommunications service providers to have contingency plans; Review provider contingency plans to ensure that the plans meet organizational contingency requirements; and Obtain evidence of contingency testing and training by providers [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.5: Alternate Telecommunication Service Testing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-8.5: Alternate Telecommunication Service Testing
Test alternate telecommunication services [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9: System Backup
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9: System Backup
Conduct backups of user-level information contained in [organization-defined parameter] [organization-defined parameter]; Conduct backups of system-level information contained in the system [organization-defined parameter]; Conduct backups of system documentation, including security- and privacy-related documentation [organization-defined parameter] ; and Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of backup information.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.1: Testing for Reliability and Integrity
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.1: Testing for Reliability and Integrity
Test backup information [organization-defined parameter] to verify media reliability and information integrity.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.2: Test Restoration Using Sampling
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.2: Test Restoration Using Sampling
Use a sample of backup information in the restoration of selected system functions as part of contingency plan testing.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.3: Separate Storage for Critical Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.3: Separate Storage for Critical Information
Store backup copies of [organization-defined parameter] in a separate facility or in a fire rated container that is not collocated with the operational system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.5: Transfer to Alternate Storage Site
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.5: Transfer to Alternate Storage Site
Transfer system backup information to the alternate storage site [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.6: Redundant Secondary System
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.6: Redundant Secondary System
Conduct system backup by maintaining a redundant secondary system that is not collocated with the primary system and that can be activated without loss of information or disruption to operations.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.7: Dual Authorization for Deletion or Destruction
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.7: Dual Authorization for Deletion or Destruction
Enforce dual authorization for the deletion or destruction of [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.8: Cryptographic Protection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-9.8: Cryptographic Protection
Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure and modification of [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-10: System Recovery and Reconstitution
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-10: System Recovery and Reconstitution
Provide for the recovery and reconstitution of the system to a known state within [organization-defined parameter] after a disruption, compromise, or failure.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-10.2: Transaction Recovery
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-10.2: Transaction Recovery
Implement transaction recovery for systems that are transaction-based.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-10.4: Restore Within Time Period
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-10.4: Restore Within Time Period
Provide the capability to restore system components within [organization-defined parameter] from configuration-controlled and integrity-protected information representing a known, operational state for the components.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-10.6: Component Protection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-10.6: Component Protection
Protect system components used for recovery and reconstitution.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-11: Alternate Communications Protocols
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-11: Alternate Communications Protocols
Provide the capability to employ [organization-defined parameter] in support of maintaining continuity of operations.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-12: Safe Mode
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-12: Safe Mode
When [organization-defined parameter] are detected, enter a safe mode of operation with [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - CP-13: Alternative Security Mechanisms
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - CP-13: Alternative Security Mechanisms
Employ [organization-defined parameter] for satisfying [organization-defined parameter] when the primary means of implementing the security function is unavailable or compromised.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] identification and authentication policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the identification and authentication policy and the associated identification and authentication controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the identification and authentication policy and procedures; and Review and update the current identification and authentication: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users and associate that unique identification with processes acting on behalf of those users.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.1: Multi-factor Authentication to Privileged Accounts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.1: Multi-factor Authentication to Privileged Accounts
Implement multi-factor authentication for access to privileged accounts.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.2: Multi-factor Authentication to Non-privileged Accounts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.2: Multi-factor Authentication to Non-privileged Accounts
Implement multi-factor authentication for access to non-privileged accounts.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.5: Individual Authentication with Group Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.5: Individual Authentication with Group Authentication
When shared accounts or authenticators are employed, require users to be individually authenticated before granting access to the shared accounts or resources.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.6: Access to Accounts —separate Device
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.6: Access to Accounts —separate Device
Implement multi-factor authentication for [organization-defined parameter] access to [organization-defined parameter] such that: One of the factors is provided by a device separate from the system gaining access; and The device meets [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.8: Access to Accounts — Replay Resistant
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.8: Access to Accounts — Replay Resistant
Implement replay-resistant authentication mechanisms for access to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.10: Single Sign-on
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.10: Single Sign-on
Provide a single sign-on capability for [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.12: Acceptance of PIV Credentials
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.12: Acceptance of PIV Credentials
Accept and electronically verify Personal Identity Verification-compliant credentials.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.13: Out-of-band Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2.13: Out-of-band Authentication
Implement the following out-of-band authentication mechanisms under [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-3: Device Identification and Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-3: Device Identification and Authentication
Uniquely identify and authenticate [organization-defined parameter] before establishing a [organization-defined parameter] connection.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-3.1: Cryptographic Bidirectional Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-3.1: Cryptographic Bidirectional Authentication
Authenticate [organization-defined parameter] before establishing [organization-defined parameter] connection using bidirectional authentication that is cryptographically based.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-3.3: Dynamic Address Allocation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-3.3: Dynamic Address Allocation
Where addresses are allocated dynamically, standardize dynamic address allocation lease information and the lease duration assigned to devices in accordance with [organization-defined parameter] ; and Audit lease information when assigned to a device.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-3.4: Device Attestation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-3.4: Device Attestation
Handle device identification and authentication based on attestation by [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4: Identifier Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4: Identifier Management
Manage system identifiers by: Receiving authorization from [organization-defined parameter] to assign an individual, group, role, service, or device identifier; Selecting an identifier that identifies an individual, group, role, service, or device; Assigning the identifier to the intended individual, group, role, service, or device; and Preventing reuse of identifiers for [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.1: Prohibit Account Identifiers as Public Identifiers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.1: Prohibit Account Identifiers as Public Identifiers
Prohibit the use of system account identifiers that are the same as public identifiers for individual accounts.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.4: Identify User Status
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.4: Identify User Status
Manage individual identifiers by uniquely identifying each individual as [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.5: Dynamic Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.5: Dynamic Management
Manage individual identifiers dynamically in accordance with [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.6: Cross-organization Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.6: Cross-organization Management
Coordinate with the following external organizations for cross-organization management of identifiers: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.8: Pairwise Pseudonymous Identifiers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.8: Pairwise Pseudonymous Identifiers
Generate pairwise pseudonymous identifiers.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.9: Attribute Maintenance and Protection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-4.9: Attribute Maintenance and Protection
Maintain the attributes for each uniquely identified individual, device, or service in [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5: Authenticator Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5: Authenticator Management
Manage system authenticators by: Verifying, as part of the initial authenticator distribution, the identity of the individual, group, role, service, or device receiving the authenticator; Establishing initial authenticator content for any authenticators issued by the organization; Ensuring that authenticators have sufficient strength of mechanism for their intended use; Establishing and implementing administrative procedures for initial authenticator distribution, for lost or compromised or damaged authenticators, and for revoking authenticators; Changing default authenticators prior to first use; Changing or refreshing authenticators [organization-defined parameter] or when [organization-defined parameter] occur; Protecting authenticator content from unauthorized disclosure and modification; Requiring individuals to take, and having devices implement, specific controls to protect authenticators; and Changing authenticators for group or role accounts when membership to those accounts changes.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.1: Password-based Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.1: Password-based Authentication
For password-based authentication: Maintain a list of commonly-used, expected, or compromised passwords and update the list [organization-defined parameter] and when organizational passwords are suspected to have been compromised directly or indirectly; Verify, when users create or update passwords, that the passwords are not found on the list of commonly-used, expected, or compromised passwords in IA-5(1)(a); Transmit passwords only over cryptographically-protected channels; Store passwords using an approved salted key derivation function, preferably using a keyed hash; Require immediate selection of a new password upon account recovery; Allow user selection of long passwords and passphrases, including spaces and all printable characters; Employ automated tools to assist the user in selecting strong password authenticators; and Enforce the following composition and complexity rules: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.2: Public Key-based Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.2: Public Key-based Authentication
For public key-based authentication: Enforce authorized access to the corresponding private key; and Map the authenticated identity to the account of the individual or group; and When public key infrastructure (PKI) is used: Validate certificates by constructing and verifying a certification path to an accepted trust anchor, including checking certificate status information; and Implement a local cache of revocation data to support path discovery and validation.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.5: Change Authenticators Prior to Delivery
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.5: Change Authenticators Prior to Delivery
Require developers and installers of system components to provide unique authenticators or change default authenticators prior to delivery and installation.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.6: Protection of Authenticators
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.6: Protection of Authenticators
Protect authenticators commensurate with the security category of the information to which use of the authenticator permits access.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.7: No Embedded Unencrypted Static Authenticators
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.7: No Embedded Unencrypted Static Authenticators
Ensure that unencrypted static authenticators are not embedded in applications or other forms of static storage.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.8: Multiple System Accounts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.8: Multiple System Accounts
Implement [organization-defined parameter] to manage the risk of compromise due to individuals having accounts on multiple systems.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.9: Federated Credential Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.9: Federated Credential Management
Use the following external organizations to federate credentials: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.10: Dynamic Credential Binding
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.10: Dynamic Credential Binding
Bind identities and authenticators dynamically using the following rules: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.12: Biometric Authentication Performance
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.12: Biometric Authentication Performance
For biometric-based authentication, employ mechanisms that satisfy the following biometric quality requirements [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.13: Expiration of Cached Authenticators
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.13: Expiration of Cached Authenticators
Prohibit the use of cached authenticators after [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.14: Managing Content of PKI Trust Stores
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.14: Managing Content of PKI Trust Stores
For PKI-based authentication, employ an organization-wide methodology for managing the content of PKI trust stores installed across all platforms, including networks, operating systems, browsers, and applications.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.15: GSA-approved Products and Services
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.15: GSA-approved Products and Services
Use only General Services Administration-approved products and services for identity, credential, and access management.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.16: In-person or Trusted External Party Authenticator Issuance
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.16: In-person or Trusted External Party Authenticator Issuance
Require that the issuance of [organization-defined parameter] be conducted [organization-defined parameter] before [organization-defined parameter] with authorization by [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.17: Presentation Attack Detection for Biometric Authenticators
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.17: Presentation Attack Detection for Biometric Authenticators
Employ presentation attack detection mechanisms for biometric-based authentication.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.18: Password Managers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-5.18: Password Managers
Employ [organization-defined parameter] to generate and manage passwords; and Protect the passwords using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-6: Authentication Feedback
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-6: Authentication Feedback
Obscure feedback of authentication information during the authentication process to protect the information from possible exploitation and use by unauthorized individuals.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-7: Cryptographic Module Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-7: Cryptographic Module Authentication
Implement mechanisms for authentication to a cryptographic module that meet the requirements of applicable laws, executive orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, and guidelines for such authentication.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8: Identification and Authentication (Non-organizational Users)
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8: Identification and Authentication (Non-organizational Users)
Uniquely identify and authenticate non-organizational users or processes acting on behalf of non-organizational users.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.1: Acceptance of PIV Credentials from Other Agencies
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.1: Acceptance of PIV Credentials from Other Agencies
Accept and electronically verify Personal Identity Verification-compliant credentials from other federal agencies.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.2: Acceptance of External Authenticators
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.2: Acceptance of External Authenticators
Accept only external authenticators that are NIST-compliant; and Document and maintain a list of accepted external authenticators.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.4: Use of Defined Profiles
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.4: Use of Defined Profiles
Conform to the following profiles for identity management [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.5: Acceptance of PIV-I Credentials
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.5: Acceptance of PIV-I Credentials
Accept and verify federated or PKI credentials that meet [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.6: Disassociability
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-8.6: Disassociability
Implement the following measures to disassociate user attributes or identifier assertion relationships among individuals, credential service providers, and relying parties: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-9: Service Identification and Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-9: Service Identification and Authentication
Uniquely identify and authenticate [organization-defined parameter] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-10: Adaptive Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-10: Adaptive Authentication
Require individuals accessing the system to employ [organization-defined parameter] under specific [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-11: Re-authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-11: Re-authentication
Require users to re-authenticate when [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12: Identity Proofing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12: Identity Proofing
Identity proof users that require accounts for logical access to systems based on appropriate identity assurance level requirements as specified in applicable standards and guidelines; Resolve user identities to a unique individual; and Collect, validate, and verify identity evidence.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.1: Supervisor Authorization
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.1: Supervisor Authorization
Require that the registration process to receive an account for logical access includes supervisor or sponsor authorization.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.2: Identity Evidence
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.2: Identity Evidence
Require evidence of individual identification be presented to the registration authority.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.3: Identity Evidence Validation and Verification
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.3: Identity Evidence Validation and Verification
Require that the presented identity evidence be validated and verified through [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.4: In-person Validation and Verification
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.4: In-person Validation and Verification
Require that the validation and verification of identity evidence be conducted in person before a designated registration authority.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.5: Address Confirmation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.5: Address Confirmation
Require that a [organization-defined parameter] be delivered through an out-of-band channel to verify the users address (physical or digital) of record.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.6: Accept Externally-proofed Identities
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-12.6: Accept Externally-proofed Identities
Accept externally-proofed identities at [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-13: Identity Providers and Authorization Servers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-13: Identity Providers and Authorization Servers
Employ identity providers and authorization servers to manage user, device, and non-person entity (NPE) identities, attributes, and access rights supporting authentication and authorization decisions in accordance with [organization-defined parameter] using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-13.1: Protection of Cryptographic Keys
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-13.1: Protection of Cryptographic Keys
Cryptographic keys that protect access tokens are generated, managed, and protected from disclosure and misuse.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-13.2: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-13.2: Verification of Identity Assertions and Access Tokens
The source and integrity of identity assertions and access tokens are verified before granting access to system and information resources.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-13.3: Token Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-13.3: Token Management
In accordance with [organization-defined parameter], assertions and access tokens are: generated; issued; refreshed; revoked; time-restricted; and audience-restricted.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] incident response policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the incident response policy and the associated incident response controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the incident response policy and procedures; and Review and update the current incident response: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-2: Incident Response Training
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-2: Incident Response Training
Provide incident response training to system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities: Within [organization-defined parameter] of assuming an incident response role or responsibility or acquiring system access; When required by system changes; and [organization-defined parameter] thereafter; and Review and update incident response training content [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-2.1: Simulated Events
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-2.1: Simulated Events
Incorporate simulated events into incident response training to facilitate the required response by personnel in crisis situations.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-2.2: Automated Training Environments
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-2.2: Automated Training Environments
Provide an incident response training environment using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-2.3: Breach
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-2.3: Breach
Provide incident response training on how to identify and respond to a breach, including the organization’s process for reporting a breach.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-3: Incident Response Testing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-3: Incident Response Testing
Test the effectiveness of the incident response capability for the system [organization-defined parameter] using the following tests: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-3.1: Automated Testing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-3.1: Automated Testing
Test the incident response capability using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-3.2: Coordination with Related Plans
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-3.2: Coordination with Related Plans
Coordinate incident response testing with organizational elements responsible for related plans.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-3.3: Continuous Improvement
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-3.3: Continuous Improvement
Use qualitative and quantitative data from testing to: Determine the effectiveness of incident response processes; Continuously improve incident response processes; and Provide incident response measures and metrics that are accurate, consistent, and in a reproducible format.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4: Incident Handling
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4: Incident Handling
Implement an incident handling capability for incidents that is consistent with the incident response plan and includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery; Coordinate incident handling activities with contingency planning activities; Incorporate lessons learned from ongoing incident handling activities into incident response procedures, training, and testing, and implement the resulting changes accordingly; and Ensure the rigor, intensity, scope, and results of incident handling activities are comparable and predictable across the organization.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.1: Automated Incident Handling Processes
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.1: Automated Incident Handling Processes
Support the incident handling process using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.2: Dynamic Reconfiguration
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.2: Dynamic Reconfiguration
Include the following types of dynamic reconfiguration for [organization-defined parameter] as part of the incident response capability: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.3: Continuity of Operations
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.3: Continuity of Operations
Identify [organization-defined parameter] and take the following actions in response to those incidents to ensure continuation of organizational mission and business functions: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.4: Information Correlation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.4: Information Correlation
Correlate incident information and individual incident responses to achieve an organization-wide perspective on incident awareness and response.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.5: Automatic Disabling of System
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.5: Automatic Disabling of System
Implement a configurable capability to automatically disable the system if [organization-defined parameter] are detected.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.6: Insider Threats
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.6: Insider Threats
Implement an incident handling capability for incidents involving insider threats.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.7: Insider Threats — Intra-organization Coordination
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.7: Insider Threats — Intra-organization Coordination
Coordinate an incident handling capability for insider threats that includes the following organizational entities [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.8: Correlation with External Organizations
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.8: Correlation with External Organizations
Coordinate with [organization-defined parameter] to correlate and share [organization-defined parameter] to achieve a cross-organization perspective on incident awareness and more effective incident responses.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.9: Dynamic Response Capability
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.9: Dynamic Response Capability
Employ [organization-defined parameter] to respond to incidents.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.10: Supply Chain Coordination
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.10: Supply Chain Coordination
Coordinate incident handling activities involving supply chain events with other organizations involved in the supply chain.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.11: Integrated Incident Response Team
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.11: Integrated Incident Response Team
Establish and maintain an integrated incident response team that can be deployed to any location identified by the organization in [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.12: Malicious Code and Forensic Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.12: Malicious Code and Forensic Analysis
Analyze malicious code and/or other residual artifacts remaining in the system after the incident.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.13: Behavior Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.13: Behavior Analysis
Analyze anomalous or suspected adversarial behavior in or related to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.14: Security Operations Center
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.14: Security Operations Center
Establish and maintain a security operations center.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.15: Public Relations and Reputation Repair
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-4.15: Public Relations and Reputation Repair
Manage public relations associated with an incident; and Employ measures to repair the reputation of the organization.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-5: Incident Monitoring
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-5: Incident Monitoring
Track and document incidents.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-5.1: Automated Tracking, Data Collection, and Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-5.1: Automated Tracking, Data Collection, and Analysis
Track incidents and collect and analyze incident information using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-6: Incident Reporting
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-6: Incident Reporting
Require personnel to report suspected incidents to the organizational incident response capability within [organization-defined parameter] ; and Report incident information to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-6.1: Automated Reporting
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-6.1: Automated Reporting
Report incidents using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-6.2: Vulnerabilities Related to Incidents
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-6.2: Vulnerabilities Related to Incidents
Report system vulnerabilities associated with reported incidents to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-6.3: Supply Chain Coordination
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-6.3: Supply Chain Coordination
Provide incident information to the provider of the product or service and other organizations involved in the supply chain or supply chain governance for systems or system components related to the incident.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-7: Incident Response Assistance
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-7: Incident Response Assistance
Provide an incident response support resource, integral to the organizational incident response capability, that offers advice and assistance to users of the system for the handling and reporting of incidents.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-7.1: Automation Support for Availability of Information and Support
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-7.1: Automation Support for Availability of Information and Support
Increase the availability of incident response information and support using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-7.2: Coordination with External Providers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-7.2: Coordination with External Providers
Establish a direct, cooperative relationship between its incident response capability and external providers of system protection capability; and Identify organizational incident response team members to the external providers.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-8: Incident Response Plan
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-8: Incident Response Plan
Develop an incident response plan that: Provides the organization with a roadmap for implementing its incident response capability; Describes the structure and organization of the incident response capability; Provides a high-level approach for how the incident response capability fits into the overall organization; Meets the unique requirements of the organization, which relate to mission, size, structure, and functions; Defines reportable incidents; Provides metrics for measuring the incident response capability within the organization; Defines the resources and management support needed to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability; Addresses the sharing of incident information; Is reviewed and approved by [organization-defined parameter] [organization-defined parameter] ; and Explicitly designates responsibility for incident response to [organization-defined parameter]. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to [organization-defined parameter]; Update the incident response plan to address system and organizational changes or problems encountered during plan implementation, execution, or testing; Communicate incident response plan changes to [organization-defined parameter] ; and Protect the incident response plan from unauthorized disclosure and modification.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-8.1: Breaches
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-8.1: Breaches
Include the following in the Incident Response Plan for breaches involving personally identifiable information: A process to determine if notice to individuals or other organizations, including oversight organizations, is needed; An assessment process to determine the extent of the harm, embarrassment, inconvenience, or unfairness to affected individuals and any mechanisms to mitigate such harms; and Identification of applicable privacy requirements.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-9: Information Spillage Response
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-9: Information Spillage Response
Respond to information spills by: Assigning [organization-defined parameter] with responsibility for responding to information spills; Identifying the specific information involved in the system contamination; Alerting [organization-defined parameter] of the information spill using a method of communication not associated with the spill; Isolating the contaminated system or system component; Eradicating the information from the contaminated system or component; Identifying other systems or system components that may have been subsequently contaminated; and Performing the following additional actions: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-9.2: Training
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-9.2: Training
Provide information spillage response training [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-9.3: Post-spill Operations
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-9.3: Post-spill Operations
Implement the following procedures to ensure that organizational personnel impacted by information spills can continue to carry out assigned tasks while contaminated systems are undergoing corrective actions: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IR-9.4: Exposure to Unauthorized Personnel
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IR-9.4: Exposure to Unauthorized Personnel
Employ the following controls for personnel exposed to information not within assigned access authorizations: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] maintenance policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the maintenance policy and the associated maintenance controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the maintenance policy and procedures; and Review and update the current maintenance: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-2: Controlled Maintenance
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-2: Controlled Maintenance
Schedule, document, and review records of maintenance, repair, and replacement on system components in accordance with manufacturer or vendor specifications and/or organizational requirements; Approve and monitor all maintenance activities, whether performed on site or remotely and whether the system or system components are serviced on site or removed to another location; Require that [organization-defined parameter] explicitly approve the removal of the system or system components from organizational facilities for off-site maintenance, repair, or replacement; Sanitize equipment to remove the following information from associated media prior to removal from organizational facilities for off-site maintenance, repair, or replacement: [organization-defined parameter]; Check all potentially impacted controls to verify that the controls are still functioning properly following maintenance, repair, or replacement actions; and Include the following information in organizational maintenance records: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-2.2: Automated Maintenance Activities
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-2.2: Automated Maintenance Activities
Schedule, conduct, and document maintenance, repair, and replacement actions for the system using [organization-defined parameter] ; and Produce up-to date, accurate, and complete records of all maintenance, repair, and replacement actions requested, scheduled, in process, and completed.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3: Maintenance Tools
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3: Maintenance Tools
Approve, control, and monitor the use of system maintenance tools; and Review previously approved system maintenance tools [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.1: Inspect Tools
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.1: Inspect Tools
Inspect the maintenance tools used by maintenance personnel for improper or unauthorized modifications.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.2: Inspect Media
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.2: Inspect Media
Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code before the media are used in the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.3: Prevent Unauthorized Removal
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.3: Prevent Unauthorized Removal
Prevent the removal of maintenance equipment containing organizational information by: Verifying that there is no organizational information contained on the equipment; Sanitizing or destroying the equipment; Retaining the equipment within the facility; or Obtaining an exemption from [organization-defined parameter] explicitly authorizing removal of the equipment from the facility.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.4: Restricted Tool Use
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.4: Restricted Tool Use
Restrict the use of maintenance tools to authorized personnel only.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.5: Execution with Privilege
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.5: Execution with Privilege
Monitor the use of maintenance tools that execute with increased privilege.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.6: Software Updates and Patches
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-3.6: Software Updates and Patches
Inspect maintenance tools to ensure the latest software updates and patches are installed.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-4: Nonlocal Maintenance
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-4: Nonlocal Maintenance
Approve and monitor nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic activities; Allow the use of nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic tools only as consistent with organizational policy and documented in the security plan for the system; Employ strong authentication in the establishment of nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; Maintain records for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic activities; and Terminate session and network connections when nonlocal maintenance is completed.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-4.1: Logging and Review
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-4.1: Logging and Review
Log [organization-defined parameter] for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; and Review the audit records of the maintenance and diagnostic sessions to detect anomalous behavior.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-4.3: Comparable Security and Sanitization
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-4.3: Comparable Security and Sanitization
Require that nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic services be performed from a system that implements a security capability comparable to the capability implemented on the system being serviced; or Remove the component to be serviced from the system prior to nonlocal maintenance or diagnostic services; sanitize the component (for organizational information); and after the service is performed, inspect and sanitize the component (for potentially malicious software) before reconnecting the component to the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-4.4: Authentication and Separation of Maintenance Sessions
chunks.text:

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5: Maintenance Personnel
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5: Maintenance Personnel
Establish a process for maintenance personnel authorization and maintain a list of authorized maintenance organizations or personnel; Verify that non-escorted personnel performing maintenance on the system possess the required access authorizations; and Designate organizational personnel with required access authorizations and technical competence to supervise the maintenance activities of personnel who do not possess the required access authorizations.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.1: Individuals Without Appropriate Access
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.1: Individuals Without Appropriate Access
Implement procedures for the use of maintenance personnel that lack appropriate security clearances or are not U.S. citizens, that include the following requirements: Maintenance personnel who do not have needed access authorizations, clearances, or formal access approvals are escorted and supervised during the performance of maintenance and diagnostic activities on the system by approved organizational personnel who are fully cleared, have appropriate access authorizations, and are technically qualified; and Prior to initiating maintenance or diagnostic activities by personnel who do not have needed access authorizations, clearances or formal access approvals, all volatile information storage components within the system are sanitized and all nonvolatile storage media are removed or physically disconnected from the system and secured; and Develop and implement [organization-defined parameter] in the event a system component cannot be sanitized, removed, or disconnected from the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.2: Security Clearances for Classified Systems
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.2: Security Clearances for Classified Systems
Verify that personnel performing maintenance and diagnostic activities on a system processing, storing, or transmitting classified information possess security clearances and formal access approvals for at least the highest classification level and for compartments of information on the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.3: Citizenship Requirements for Classified Systems
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.3: Citizenship Requirements for Classified Systems
Verify that personnel performing maintenance and diagnostic activities on a system processing, storing, or transmitting classified information are U.S. citizens.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.4: Foreign Nationals
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.4: Foreign Nationals
Ensure that: Foreign nationals with appropriate security clearances are used to conduct maintenance and diagnostic activities on classified systems only when the systems are jointly owned and operated by the United States and foreign allied governments, or owned and operated solely by foreign allied governments; and Approvals, consents, and detailed operational conditions regarding the use of foreign nationals to conduct maintenance and diagnostic activities on classified systems are fully documented within Memoranda of Agreements.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.5: Non-system Maintenance

## NIST SP 800-53 - MA-5.5: Non-system Maintenance

Enforce physical access authorizations at [organization-defined parameter] by: Verifying individual access authorizations before granting access to the facility; and Controlling ingress and egress to the facility using [organization-defined parameter]; Maintain physical access audit logs for [organization-defined parameter]; Control access to areas within the facility designated as publicly accessible by implementing the following controls: [organization-defined parameter]; Escort visitors and control visitor activity [organization-defined parameter]; Secure keys, combinations, and other physical access devices; Inventory [organization-defined parameter] every [organization-defined parameter] ; and Change combinations and keys [organization-defined parameter] and/or when keys are lost, combinations are compromised, or when individuals possessing the keys or combinations are transferred or terminated.
