Require that providers of external system services comply with organizational security and privacy requirements and employ the following controls: [organization-defined parameter]; Define and document organizational oversight and user roles and responsibilities with regard to external system services; and Employ the following processes, methods, and techniques to monitor control compliance by external service providers on an ongoing basis: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.1: Risk Assessments and Organizational Approvals
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.1: Risk Assessments and Organizational Approvals
Conduct an organizational assessment of risk prior to the acquisition or outsourcing of information security services; and Verify that the acquisition or outsourcing of dedicated information security services is approved by [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.2: Identification of Functions, Ports, Protocols, and Services
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.2: Identification of Functions, Ports, Protocols, and Services
Require providers of the following external system services to identify the functions, ports, protocols, and other services required for the use of such services: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.3: Establish and Maintain Trust Relationship with Providers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.3: Establish and Maintain Trust Relationship with Providers
Establish, document, and maintain trust relationships with external service providers based on the following requirements, properties, factors, or conditions: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.4: Consistent Interests of Consumers and Providers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.4: Consistent Interests of Consumers and Providers
Take the following actions to verify that the interests of [organization-defined parameter] are consistent with and reflect organizational interests: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.5: Processing, Storage, and Service Location
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.5: Processing, Storage, and Service Location
Restrict the location of [organization-defined parameter] to [organization-defined parameter] based on [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.6: Organization-controlled Cryptographic Keys
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.6: Organization-controlled Cryptographic Keys
Maintain exclusive control of cryptographic keys for encrypted material stored or transmitted through an external system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.7: Organization-controlled Integrity Checking
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.7: Organization-controlled Integrity Checking
Provide the capability to check the integrity of information while it resides in the external system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.8: Processing and Storage Location — U.S. Jurisdiction
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-9.8: Processing and Storage Location — U.S. Jurisdiction
Restrict the geographic location of information processing and data storage to facilities located within in the legal jurisdictional boundary of the United States.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10: Developer Configuration Management
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10: Developer Configuration Management
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to: Perform configuration management during system, component, or service [organization-defined parameter]; Document, manage, and control the integrity of changes to [organization-defined parameter]; Implement only organization-approved changes to the system, component, or service; Document approved changes to the system, component, or service and the potential security and privacy impacts of such changes; and Track security flaws and flaw resolution within the system, component, or service and report findings to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.1: Software and Firmware Integrity Verification
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.1: Software and Firmware Integrity Verification
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to enable integrity verification of software and firmware components.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.2: Alternative Configuration Management Processes
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.2: Alternative Configuration Management Processes
Provide an alternate configuration management process using organizational personnel in the absence of a dedicated developer configuration management team.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.3: Hardware Integrity Verification
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.3: Hardware Integrity Verification
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to enable integrity verification of hardware components.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.4: Trusted Generation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.4: Trusted Generation
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to employ tools for comparing newly generated versions of security-relevant hardware descriptions, source code, and object code with previous versions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.5: Mapping Integrity for Version Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.5: Mapping Integrity for Version Control
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to maintain the integrity of the mapping between the master build data describing the current version of security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware and the on-site master copy of the data for the current version.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.6: Trusted Distribution
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.6: Trusted Distribution
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to execute procedures for ensuring that security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware updates distributed to the organization are exactly as specified by the master copies.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.7: Security and Privacy Representatives
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-10.7: Security and Privacy Representatives
Require [organization-defined parameter] to be included in the [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11: Developer Testing and Evaluation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11: Developer Testing and Evaluation
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service, at all post-design stages of the system development life cycle, to: Develop and implement a plan for ongoing security and privacy control assessments; Perform [organization-defined parameter] testing/evaluation [organization-defined parameter] at [organization-defined parameter]; Produce evidence of the execution of the assessment plan and the results of the testing and evaluation; Implement a verifiable flaw remediation process; and Correct flaws identified during testing and evaluation.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.1: Static Code Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.1: Static Code Analysis
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to employ static code analysis tools to identify common flaws and document the results of the analysis.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.2: Threat Modeling and Vulnerability Analyses
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.2: Threat Modeling and Vulnerability Analyses
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to perform threat modeling and vulnerability analyses during development and the subsequent testing and evaluation of the system, component, or service that: Uses the following contextual information: [organization-defined parameter]; Employs the following tools and methods: [organization-defined parameter]; Conducts the modeling and analyses at the following level of rigor: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Produces evidence that meets the following acceptance criteria: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.3: Independent Verification of Assessment Plans and Evidence
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.3: Independent Verification of Assessment Plans and Evidence
Require an independent agent satisfying [organization-defined parameter] to verify the correct implementation of the developer security and privacy assessment plans and the evidence produced during testing and evaluation; and Verify that the independent agent is provided with sufficient information to complete the verification process or granted the authority to obtain such information.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.4: Manual Code Reviews
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.4: Manual Code Reviews
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to perform a manual code review of [organization-defined parameter] using the following processes, procedures, and/or techniques: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.5: Penetration Testing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.5: Penetration Testing
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to perform penetration testing: At the following level of rigor: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Under the following constraints: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.6: Attack Surface Reviews
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.6: Attack Surface Reviews
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to perform attack surface reviews.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.7: Verify Scope of Testing and Evaluation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.7: Verify Scope of Testing and Evaluation
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to verify that the scope of testing and evaluation provides complete coverage of the required controls at the following level of rigor: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.8: Dynamic Code Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.8: Dynamic Code Analysis
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to employ dynamic code analysis tools to identify common flaws and document the results of the analysis.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.9: Interactive Application Security Testing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-11.9: Interactive Application Security Testing
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to employ interactive application security testing tools to identify flaws and document the results.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15: Development Process, Standards, and Tools
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15: Development Process, Standards, and Tools
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to follow a documented development process that: Explicitly addresses security and privacy requirements; Identifies the standards and tools used in the development process; Documents the specific tool options and tool configurations used in the development process; and Documents, manages, and ensures the integrity of changes to the process and/or tools used in development; and Review the development process, standards, tools, tool options, and tool configurations [organization-defined parameter] to determine if the process, standards, tools, tool options and tool configurations selected and employed can satisfy the following security and privacy requirements: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.1: Quality Metrics
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.1: Quality Metrics
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to: Define quality metrics at the beginning of the development process; and Provide evidence of meeting the quality metrics [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.2: Security and Privacy Tracking Tools
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.2: Security and Privacy Tracking Tools
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to select and employ security and privacy tracking tools for use during the development process.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.3: Criticality Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.3: Criticality Analysis
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to perform a criticality analysis: At the following decision points in the system development life cycle: [organization-defined parameter] ; and At the following level of rigor: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.5: Attack Surface Reduction
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.5: Attack Surface Reduction
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to reduce attack surfaces to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.6: Continuous Improvement
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.6: Continuous Improvement
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to implement an explicit process to continuously improve the development process.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.7: Automated Vulnerability Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.7: Automated Vulnerability Analysis
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service [organization-defined parameter] to: Perform an automated vulnerability analysis using [organization-defined parameter]; Determine the exploitation potential for discovered vulnerabilities; Determine potential risk mitigations for delivered vulnerabilities; and Deliver the outputs of the tools and results of the analysis to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.8: Reuse of Threat and Vulnerability Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.8: Reuse of Threat and Vulnerability Information
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to use threat modeling and vulnerability analyses from similar systems, components, or services to inform the current development process.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.10: Incident Response Plan
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.10: Incident Response Plan
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to provide, implement, and test an incident response plan.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.11: Archive System or Component
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.11: Archive System or Component
Require the developer of the system or system component to archive the system or component to be released or delivered together with the corresponding evidence supporting the final security and privacy review.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.12: Minimize Personally Identifiable Information
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.12: Minimize Personally Identifiable Information
Require the developer of the system or system component to minimize the use of personally identifiable information in development and test environments.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.13: Logging Syntax
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-15.13: Logging Syntax
Require the developer of the system or system component to minimize the use of personally identifiable information in development and test environments.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-16: Developer-provided Training
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-16: Developer-provided Training
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to provide the following training on the correct use and operation of the implemented security and privacy functions, controls, and/or mechanisms: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17: Developer Security and Privacy Architecture and Design
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17: Developer Security and Privacy Architecture and Design
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to produce a design specification and security and privacy architecture that: Is consistent with the organization’s security and privacy architecture that is an integral part the organization’s enterprise architecture; Accurately and completely describes the required security and privacy functionality, and the allocation of controls among physical and logical components; and Expresses how individual security and privacy functions, mechanisms, and services work together to provide required security and privacy capabilities and a unified approach to protection.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.1: Formal Policy Model
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.1: Formal Policy Model
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to: Produce, as an integral part of the development process, a formal policy model describing the [organization-defined parameter] to be enforced; and Prove that the formal policy model is internally consistent and sufficient to enforce the defined elements of the organizational security and privacy policy when implemented.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.2: Security-relevant Components
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.2: Security-relevant Components
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to: Define security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware; and Provide a rationale that the definition for security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware is complete.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.3: Formal Correspondence
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.3: Formal Correspondence
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to: Produce, as an integral part of the development process, a formal top-level specification that specifies the interfaces to security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware in terms of exceptions, error messages, and effects; Show via proof to the extent feasible with additional informal demonstration as necessary, that the formal top-level specification is consistent with the formal policy model; Show via informal demonstration, that the formal top-level specification completely covers the interfaces to security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware; Show that the formal top-level specification is an accurate description of the implemented security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware; and Describe the security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware mechanisms not addressed in the formal top-level specification but strictly internal to the security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.4: Informal Correspondence
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.4: Informal Correspondence
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to: Produce, as an integral part of the development process, an informal descriptive top-level specification that specifies the interfaces to security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware in terms of exceptions, error messages, and effects; Show via [organization-defined parameter] that the descriptive top-level specification is consistent with the formal policy model; Show via informal demonstration, that the descriptive top-level specification completely covers the interfaces to security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware; Show that the descriptive top-level specification is an accurate description of the interfaces to security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware; and Describe the security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware mechanisms not addressed in the descriptive top-level specification but strictly internal to the security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.5: Conceptually Simple Design
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.5: Conceptually Simple Design
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to: Design and structure the security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware to use a complete, conceptually simple protection mechanism with precisely defined semantics; and Internally structure the security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware with specific regard for this mechanism.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.6: Structure for Testing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.6: Structure for Testing
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to structure security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware to facilitate testing.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.7: Structure for Least Privilege
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.7: Structure for Least Privilege
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to structure security-relevant hardware, software, and firmware to facilitate controlling access with least privilege.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.8: Orchestration
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.8: Orchestration
Design [organization-defined parameter] with coordinated behavior to implement the following capabilities: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.9: Design Diversity
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-17.9: Design Diversity
Use different designs for [organization-defined parameter] to satisfy a common set of requirements or to provide equivalent functionality.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-20: Customized Development of Critical Components
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-20: Customized Development of Critical Components
Reimplement or custom develop the following critical system components: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-21: Developer Screening
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-21: Developer Screening
Require that the developer of [organization-defined parameter]: Has appropriate access authorizations as determined by assigned [organization-defined parameter] ; and Satisfies the following additional personnel screening criteria: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-22: Unsupported System Components
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-22: Unsupported System Components
Replace system components when support for the components is no longer available from the developer, vendor, or manufacturer; or Provide the following options for alternative sources for continued support for unsupported components [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-23: Specialization
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-23: Specialization
Employ [organization-defined parameter] on [organization-defined parameter] supporting mission essential services or functions to increase the trustworthiness in those systems or components.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SA-24: Design For Cyber Resiliency
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SA-24: Design For Cyber Resiliency
Design organizational systems, system components, or system services to achieve cyber resiliency by: Defining the following cyber resiliency goals: [organization-defined parameter]. Defining the following cyber resiliency objectives: [organization-defined parameter]. Defining the following cyber resiliency techniques: [organization-defined parameter]. Defining the following cyber resiliency implementation approaches: [organization-defined parameter]. Defining the following cyber resiliency design principles: [organization-defined parameter]. Implement the selected cyber resiliency goals, objectives, techniques, implementation approaches, and design principles as part of an organizational risk management process or systems security engineering process.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] system and communications protection policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the system and communications protection policy and the associated system and communications protection controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the system and communications protection policy and procedures; and Review and update the current system and communications protection: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-2: Separation of System and User Functionality
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-2: Separation of System and User Functionality
Separate user functionality, including user interface services, from system management functionality.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-2.1: Interfaces for Non-privileged Users
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-2.1: Interfaces for Non-privileged Users
Prevent the presentation of system management functionality at interfaces to non-privileged users.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-2.2: Disassociability
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-2.2: Disassociability
Store state information from applications and software separately.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3: Security Function Isolation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3: Security Function Isolation
Isolate security functions from nonsecurity functions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.1: Hardware Separation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.1: Hardware Separation
Employ hardware separation mechanisms to implement security function isolation.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.2: Access and Flow Control Functions
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.2: Access and Flow Control Functions
Isolate security functions enforcing access and information flow control from nonsecurity functions and from other security functions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.3: Minimize Nonsecurity Functionality
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.3: Minimize Nonsecurity Functionality
Minimize the number of nonsecurity functions included within the isolation boundary containing security functions.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.4: Module Coupling and Cohesiveness
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.4: Module Coupling and Cohesiveness
Implement security functions as largely independent modules that maximize internal cohesiveness within modules and minimize coupling between modules.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.5: Layered Structures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-3.5: Layered Structures
Implement security functions as a layered structure minimizing interactions between layers of the design and avoiding any dependence by lower layers on the functionality or correctness of higher layers.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-4: Information in Shared System Resources
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-4: Information in Shared System Resources
Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-4.2: Multilevel or Periods Processing
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-4.2: Multilevel or Periods Processing
Prevent unauthorized information transfer via shared resources in accordance with [organization-defined parameter] when system processing explicitly switches between different information classification levels or security categories.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-5: Denial-of-service Protection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-5: Denial-of-service Protection
[organization-defined parameter] the effects of the following types of denial-of-service events: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Employ the following controls to achieve the denial-of-service objective: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-5.1: Restrict Ability to Attack Other Systems
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-5.1: Restrict Ability to Attack Other Systems
Restrict the ability of individuals to launch the following denial-of-service attacks against other systems: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-5.2: Capacity, Bandwidth, and Redundancy
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-5.2: Capacity, Bandwidth, and Redundancy
Manage capacity, bandwidth, or other redundancy to limit the effects of information flooding denial-of-service attacks.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-5.3: Detection and Monitoring
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-5.3: Detection and Monitoring
Employ the following monitoring tools to detect indicators of denial-of-service attacks against, or launched from, the system: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Monitor the following system resources to determine if sufficient resources exist to prevent effective denial-of-service attacks: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-6: Resource Availability
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-6: Resource Availability
Protect the availability of resources by allocating [organization-defined parameter] by [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7: Boundary Protection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7: Boundary Protection
Monitor and control communications at the external managed interfaces to the system and at key internal managed interfaces within the system; Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are [organization-defined parameter] separated from internal organizational networks; and Connect to external networks or systems only through managed interfaces consisting of boundary protection devices arranged in accordance with an organizational security and privacy architecture.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.3: Access Points
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.3: Access Points
Limit the number of external network connections to the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.4: External Telecommunications Services
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.4: External Telecommunications Services
Implement a managed interface for each external telecommunication service; Establish a traffic flow policy for each managed interface; Protect the confidentiality and integrity of the information being transmitted across each interface; Document each exception to the traffic flow policy with a supporting mission or business need and duration of that need; Review exceptions to the traffic flow policy [organization-defined parameter] and remove exceptions that are no longer supported by an explicit mission or business need; Prevent unauthorized exchange of control plane traffic with external networks; Publish information to enable remote networks to detect unauthorized control plane traffic from internal networks; and Filter unauthorized control plane traffic from external networks.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.5: Deny by Default — Allow by Exception
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.5: Deny by Default — Allow by Exception
Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.7: Split Tunneling for Remote Devices
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.7: Split Tunneling for Remote Devices
Prevent split tunneling for remote devices connecting to organizational systems unless the split tunnel is securely provisioned using [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.8: Route Traffic to Authenticated Proxy Servers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.8: Route Traffic to Authenticated Proxy Servers
Route [organization-defined parameter] to [organization-defined parameter] through authenticated proxy servers at managed interfaces.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.9: Restrict Threatening Outgoing Communications Traffic
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.9: Restrict Threatening Outgoing Communications Traffic
Detect and deny outgoing communications traffic posing a threat to external systems; and Audit the identity of internal users associated with denied communications.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.10: Prevent Exfiltration
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.10: Prevent Exfiltration
Prevent the exfiltration of information; and Conduct exfiltration tests [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.11: Restrict Incoming Communications Traffic
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.11: Restrict Incoming Communications Traffic
Only allow incoming communications from [organization-defined parameter] to be routed to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.12: Host-based Protection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.12: Host-based Protection
Implement [organization-defined parameter] at [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.13: Isolation of Security Tools, Mechanisms, and Support Components
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.13: Isolation of Security Tools, Mechanisms, and Support Components
Isolate [organization-defined parameter] from other internal system components by implementing physically separate subnetworks with managed interfaces to other components of the system.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.14: Protect Against Unauthorized Physical Connections
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.14: Protect Against Unauthorized Physical Connections
Protect against unauthorized physical connections at [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.15: Networked Privileged Accesses
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SC-7.15: Networked Privileged Accesses

Correct or delete personally identifiable information upon request by individuals or their designated representatives.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-18.5: Notice of Correction or Deletion
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-18.5: Notice of Correction or Deletion
Notify [organization-defined parameter] and individuals that the personally identifiable information has been corrected or deleted.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19: De-identification
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19: De-identification
Remove the following elements of personally identifiable information from datasets: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Evaluate [organization-defined parameter] for effectiveness of de-identification.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.1: Collection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.1: Collection
De-identify the dataset upon collection by not collecting personally identifiable information.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.2: Archiving
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.2: Archiving
Prohibit archiving of personally identifiable information elements if those elements in a dataset will not be needed after the dataset is archived.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.3: Release
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.3: Release
Remove personally identifiable information elements from a dataset prior to its release if those elements in the dataset do not need to be part of the data release.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.4: Removal, Masking, Encryption, Hashing, or Replacement of Direct Identifiers
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.4: Removal, Masking, Encryption, Hashing, or Replacement of Direct Identifiers
Remove, mask, encrypt, hash, or replace direct identifiers in a dataset.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.5: Statistical Disclosure Control
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.5: Statistical Disclosure Control
Manipulate numerical data, contingency tables, and statistical findings so that no individual or organization is identifiable in the results of the analysis.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.6: Differential Privacy
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.6: Differential Privacy
Prevent disclosure of personally identifiable information by adding non-deterministic noise to the results of mathematical operations before the results are reported.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.7: Validated Algorithms and Software
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.7: Validated Algorithms and Software
Perform de-identification using validated algorithms and software that is validated to implement the algorithms.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.8: Motivated Intruder
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-19.8: Motivated Intruder
Perform a motivated intruder test on the de-identified dataset to determine if the identified data remains or if the de-identified data can be re-identified.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-20: Tainting
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-20: Tainting
Embed data or capabilities in the following systems or system components to determine if organizational data has been exfiltrated or improperly removed from the organization: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-21: Information Refresh
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-21: Information Refresh
Refresh [organization-defined parameter] at [organization-defined parameter] or generate the information on demand and delete the information when no longer needed.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-22: Information Diversity
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-22: Information Diversity
Identify the following alternative sources of information for [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Use an alternative information source for the execution of essential functions or services on [organization-defined parameter] when the primary source of information is corrupted or unavailable.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-23: Information Fragmentation
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-23: Information Fragmentation
Based on [organization-defined parameter]: Fragment the following information: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Distribute the fragmented information across the following systems or system components: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-1: Policy and Procedures
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-1: Policy and Procedures
Develop, document, and disseminate to [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter] supply chain risk management policy that: Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the supply chain risk management policy and the associated supply chain risk management controls; Designate an [organization-defined parameter] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the supply chain risk management policy and procedures; and Review and update the current supply chain risk management: Policy [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter] ; and Procedures [organization-defined parameter] and following [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-2: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-2: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
Develop a plan for managing supply chain risks associated with the research and development, design, manufacturing, acquisition, delivery, integration, operations and maintenance, and disposal of the following systems, system components or system services: [organization-defined parameter]; Review and update the supply chain risk management plan [organization-defined parameter] or as required, to address threat, organizational or environmental changes; and Protect the supply chain risk management plan from unauthorized disclosure and modification.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-2.1: Establish SCRM Team
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-2.1: Establish SCRM Team
Establish a supply chain risk management team consisting of [organization-defined parameter] to lead and support the following SCRM activities: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-3: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-3: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
Establish a process or processes to identify and address weaknesses or deficiencies in the supply chain elements and processes of [organization-defined parameter] in coordination with [organization-defined parameter]; Employ the following controls to protect against supply chain risks to the system, system component, or system service and to limit the harm or consequences from supply chain-related events: [organization-defined parameter] ; and Document the selected and implemented supply chain processes and controls in [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-3.1: Diverse Supply Base
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-3.1: Diverse Supply Base
Employ a diverse set of sources for the following system components and services: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-3.2: Limitation of Harm
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-3.2: Limitation of Harm
Employ the following controls to limit harm from potential adversaries identifying and targeting the organizational supply chain: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-3.3: Sub-tier Flow Down
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-3.3: Sub-tier Flow Down
Ensure that the controls included in the contracts of prime contractors are also included in the contracts of subcontractors.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4: Provenance
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4: Provenance
Document, monitor, and maintain valid provenance of the following systems, system components, and associated data: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4.1: Identity
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4.1: Identity
Establish and maintain unique identification of the following supply chain elements, processes, and personnel associated with the identified system and critical system components: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4.2: Track and Trace
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4.2: Track and Trace
Establish and maintain unique identification of the following systems and critical system components for tracking through the supply chain: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4.3: Validate as Genuine and Not Altered
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4.3: Validate as Genuine and Not Altered
Employ the following controls to validate that the system or system component received is genuine and has not been altered: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4.4: Supply Chain Integrity — Pedigree
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-4.4: Supply Chain Integrity — Pedigree
Employ [organization-defined parameter] and conduct [organization-defined parameter] to ensure the integrity of the system and system components by validating the internal composition and provenance of critical or mission-essential technologies, products, and services.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-5: Acquisition Strategies, Tools, and Methods
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-5: Acquisition Strategies, Tools, and Methods
Employ the following acquisition strategies, contract tools, and procurement methods to protect against, identify, and mitigate supply chain risks: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-5.1: Adequate Supply
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-5.1: Adequate Supply
Employ the following controls to ensure an adequate supply of [organization-defined parameter]: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-5.2: Assessments Prior to Selection, Acceptance, Modification, or Update
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-5.2: Assessments Prior to Selection, Acceptance, Modification, or Update
Assess the system, system component, or system service prior to selection, acceptance, modification, or update.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-6: Supplier Assessments and Reviews
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-6: Supplier Assessments and Reviews
Assess and review the supply chain-related risks associated with suppliers or contractors and the system, system component, or system service they provide [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-6.1: Testing and Analysis
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-6.1: Testing and Analysis
Employ [organization-defined parameter] of the following supply chain elements, processes, and actors associated with the system, system component, or system service: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-7: Supply Chain Operations Security
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-7: Supply Chain Operations Security
Employ the following Operations Security (OPSEC) controls to protect supply chain-related information for the system, system component, or system service: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-8: Notification Agreements
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-8: Notification Agreements
Establish agreements and procedures with entities involved in the supply chain for the system, system component, or system service for the [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-9: Tamper Resistance and Detection
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-9: Tamper Resistance and Detection
Implement a tamper protection program for the system, system component, or system service.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-9.1: Multiple Stages of System Development Life Cycle
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-9.1: Multiple Stages of System Development Life Cycle
Employ anti-tamper technologies, tools, and techniques throughout the system development life cycle.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-10: Inspection of Systems or Components
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-10: Inspection of Systems or Components
Inspect the following systems or system components [organization-defined parameter] to detect tampering: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-11: Component Authenticity
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-11: Component Authenticity
Develop and implement anti-counterfeit policy and procedures that include the means to detect and prevent counterfeit components from entering the system; and Report counterfeit system components to [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-11.1: Anti-counterfeit Training
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-11.1: Anti-counterfeit Training
Train [organization-defined parameter] to detect counterfeit system components (including hardware, software, and firmware).

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-11.2: Configuration Control for Component Service and Repair
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-11.2: Configuration Control for Component Service and Repair
Maintain configuration control over the following system components awaiting service or repair and serviced or repaired components awaiting return to service: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-11.3: Anti-counterfeit Scanning
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-11.3: Anti-counterfeit Scanning
Scan for counterfeit system components [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SR-12: Component Disposal
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SR-12: Component Disposal
Dispose of [organization-defined parameter] using the following techniques and methods: [organization-defined parameter].

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AC-7: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
The system shall enforce a limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user during a defined time period. After the limit, the system shall automatically lock the account/node or delay the next logon prompt. Repeated failed SSH logons from a single source within a short window indicates a control gap here.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2: Identification and Authentication
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - IA-2: Identification and Authentication
Organizational users (including privileged users) must be uniquely identified and authenticated. Direct root login over SSH, or password-based auth for privileged accounts, violates this control. Key-based auth with MFA is required for privileged access.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6: Audit Review, Analysis, and Reporting
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - AU-6: Audit Review, Analysis, and Reporting
Organizations must review and analyze system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity on a defined frequency, and report findings to designated personnel.

chunks.title: NIST SP 800-53 - SI-4: System Monitoring
chunks.text:
## NIST SP 800-53 - SI-4: System Monitoring
The system must monitor for attacks, indicators of potential attacks, and unauthorized connections, including inbound/outbound traffic monitoring at external boundaries (e.g. repeated SYN scans to unusual ports).

chunks.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 - A.8.9: Configuration Management
chunks.text:
## ISO/IEC 27001:2022 - A.8.9: Configuration Management
Configurations, including security configurations of hardware, software, and networks, shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored, and reviewed. Containers/images running as root, or with world-writable permissions (e.g. chmod 777), are a configuration management gap.

chunks.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 - A.8.8: Management of Technical Vulnerabilities
chunks.text:
## ISO/IEC 27001:2022 - A.8.8: Management of Technical Vulnerabilities
Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained in a timely fashion, the organization's exposure evaluated, and appropriate measures taken. Unpatched/outdated dependencies with known CVEs fall under this control.

chunks.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 - A.8.16: Monitoring Activities
chunks.text:
## ISO/IEC 27001:2022 - A.8.16: Monitoring Activities
Networks, systems, and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behavior and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents. Path traversal attempts, probing for /wp-admin or /.env, and other reconnaissance patterns are indicators requiring review.

chunks.title: SOC 2 - CC6.1: Logical Access Controls
chunks.text:
## SOC 2 - CC6.1: Logical Access Controls
The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures to protect information assets from security events, including restricting privileged access and requiring strong authentication for administrative functions.

chunks.title: SOC 2 - CC7.2: System Monitoring for Security Events
chunks.text:
## SOC 2 - CC7.2: System Monitoring for Security Events
The entity monitors system components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors, and evaluates whether they represent security incidents.
